segments.text: Don’t judge, so that you won’t be judged.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For with whatever judgment you judge, you will be judged; and with whatever measure you measure, it will be measured to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Why do you see the speck that is in your brother’s eye, but don’t consider the beam that is in your own eye?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Or how will you tell your brother, ‘Let me remove the speck from your eye,’ and behold, the beam is in your own eye?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You hypocrite! First remove the beam out of your own eye, and then you can see clearly to remove the speck out of your brother’s eye.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Don’t give that which is holy to the dogs, neither throw your pearls before the pigs, lest perhaps they trample them under their feet, and turn and tear you to pieces.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Ask, and it will be given you. Seek, and you will find. Knock, and it will be opened for you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For everyone who asks receives. He who seeks finds. To him who knocks it will be opened.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Or who is there among you who, if his son asks him for bread, will give him a stone?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Or if he asks for a fish, who will give him a serpent?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you then, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father who is in heaven give good things to those who ask him!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore, whatever you desire for men to do to you, you shall also do to them; for this is the law and the prophets.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Enter in by the narrow gate; for the gate is wide and the way is broad that leads to destruction, and there are many who enter in by it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: How narrow is the gate and the way is restricted that leads to life! There are few who find it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Beware of false prophets, who come to you in sheep’s clothing, but inwardly are ravening wolves.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: By their fruits you will know them. Do you gather grapes from thorns or figs from thistles?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Even so, every good tree produces good fruit, but the corrupt tree produces evil fruit.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: A good tree can’t produce evil fruit, neither can a corrupt tree produce good fruit.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Every tree that doesn’t grow good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore by their fruits you will know them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Not everyone who says to me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter into the Kingdom of Heaven, but he who does the will of my Father who is in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Many will tell me in that day, ‘Lord, Lord, didn’t we prophesy in your name, in your name cast out demons, and in your name do many mighty works?’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then I will tell them, ‘I never knew you. Depart from me, you who work iniquity.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Everyone therefore who hears these words of mine and does them, I will liken him to a wise man who built his house on the rock.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The rain came down, the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house; and it didn’t fall, for it was founded on the rock.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Everyone who hears these words of mine and doesn’t do them will be like a foolish man who built his house on the sand.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The rain came down, the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house; and it fell—and its fall was great.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When Jesus had finished saying these things, the multitudes were astonished at his teaching,
for he taught them with authority, and not like the scribes.
